steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers argue that "invasive" and "native" are labels for temporary circumstances — climate change shifts species ranges and creates new habitats. The correct answer must apply this logic to Canada's classification of the round goby as invasive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: References a past state not mentioned in the passage — the researchers' argument is about the future, not a prior classification. ✗
  • Choice B: Canada's "invasive" label is appropriate now but may become obsolete as climate change reshapes habitats — directly applying the researchers' logic. ✓
  • Choice C: Suggests the invasive/native distinction itself is invalid, but the passage only says the labels are temporary, not meaningless. ✗
  • Choice D: Contradicts the climate-change logic by implying the label is permanent or fixed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
